title: Mirabilis micrantha
taxonomy: Mirabilis micrantha is a species of flowering plant in the family Nyctaginaceae. It is classified in the genus Mirabilis, which contains about 10 species of four-o'clock plants. The genus Mirabilis is further classified in the order Caryophyllales and the class Magnoliopsida. This classification reflects the plant's unique characteristics and evolutionary relationships within the plant kingdom. The species is native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico.
uses: Mirabilis micrantha has been used in traditional medicine in South America, where it is believed to have antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antipyretic properties. The plant's leaves and stems are used to treat various ailments, including fever, wounds, and skin conditions. In some regions, it is also used to treat respiratory issues, such as coughs and colds. The antiseptic properties of Mirabilis micrantha make it a popular remedy for wound care and preventing infections. However, more research is needed to fully understand the plant's medicinal uses and potential side effects.
benefits: Mirabilis micrantha, also known as dwarf four o'clock, is a plant species that has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits. The plant is believed to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help in reducing inflammation and protecting cells from damage. Its extracts have been studied for their potential to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and digestive issues. The plant's leaves and roots are commonly used in herbal remedies to promote overall health and well-being. Further research is needed to confirm the full extent of Mirabilis micrantha's health benefits and its potential applications in modern medicine.
constituents: Mirabilis micrantha contains various active constituents, including flavonoids and alkaloids. These compounds are responsible for its medicinal properties, such as antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects. The specific alkaloids present in Mirabilis micrantha, like berberine and palmatine, contribute to its antimicrobial and cardiovascular benefits. Additionally, flavonoids like quercetin and kaempferol may help protect against oxidative stress and cell damage. Further research is necessary to fully understand the active constituents and their potential applications in medicine.

intro: Mirabilis micrantha, also known as small four o'clock, is a species of flowering plant in the four o'clock family (Nyctaginaceae). This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which may aid in the treatment of various infections and inflammatory conditions. The bioactive constituents of Mirabilis micrantha include iridoids, flavonoids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Mirabilis micrantha is often prepared as a decoction, infusion, or tincture to utilize its bioactive compounds and alleviate various health issues.
